definitions.text: To physically place (sth or sb swh).
examples: She put her books on the table.
The police put him in a cell.

definitions.text: To place in abstract; to attach or attribute; to assign.
examples: The government put restrictions on vehicle imports.
I put £100 on the winning horse.

definitions.text: To bring or set (into a certain relation, state or condition).
examples: He is putting all his energy into this one task.

definitions.text: Ellipsis of put option (“right to sell something at a predetermined price”)
examples: He bought a January '08 put for Procter and Gamble at 80 to hedge his bet.

definitions.text: The act of putting; an action; a movement; a thrust; a push.
A fellow, especially an eccentric or elderly one; a duffer.
